title: ASC Application Manifest (asc.yaml)
description: Manifest of a single ASC application. A package repository must have exactly one asc.yaml OR one asc.stack.yaml at its root.

type.description: How the app runs: docker container, native app (systemd) or system utility

settings.description: Relative path to the app settings file (asc.settings.yaml), see asc.settings.schema.json. Environment variables, published ports and volumes are all declared there (settings with env:, type: ports, type: volumes), not in this manifest

image.description: Prebuilt Docker image to pull (type: docker). May be given alongside image-build, in which case the installer offers a choice (asc install --image | --build)

image-build.description: Build the Docker image locally from a Dockerfile in the package instead of (or beside) pulling a prebuilt 'image' (type: docker)

context.description: Build context directory, relative to the manifest (default '.')

dockerfile.description: Dockerfile path, relative to the build context (default 'Dockerfile')

args.description: Build arguments passed to the Engine (--build-arg)

stdin.description: Keep the container's stdin open (like docker run -i) so console input reaches the app (type: docker)

tty.description: Allocate a pseudo-TTY (like docker run -t) for apps whose console expects a terminal (type: docker)
